title: Primate Hair Cortisol Processing
author: Kimberley A Phillips
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bw8wphxe

steps.title: Hair clippers should be cleaned with...
steps.instruction: Hair clippers should be cleaned with alcohol before and after each use.

steps.title: Shave the hair from the upper...
steps.instruction: Shave the hair from the upper back of the animal using electric clippers.

steps.title: The hair should be shaved close...
steps.instruction: The hair should be shaved close without nicking the skin, as this can cause falsely elevated concentration of cortisol due to blood being present in the sample. It is essential that hair be collected from the same body region across all animals for a given study.

steps.title: The distal end of each sample...
steps.instruction: The distal end of each sample is then wrapped in masking tape, leaving 1–1.5 cm of the proximal hair exposed. The sample can be placed in an envelope and stored at -20 °C (-4 °F) or room temperature out of direct sunlight until further processing.

steps.title: Envelopes should be labelled with species...
steps.instruction: Envelopes should be labelled with species name, subject ID, sex, DOB (date of birth), DOC (date of collection), location of hair sample, and project affiliation code.

steps.title: Calibrate the analytical scale
steps.instruction: Calibrate the analytical scale.

steps.title: Clean instruments (forceps, spatula) with isopropyl...
steps.instruction: Clean instruments (forceps, spatula) with isopropyl alcohol wipes before and after each use.

steps.title: Cut a 7cm x 7 cm...
steps.instruction: Cut a 7cm x 7 cm square from printer paper for each hair sample.

steps.title: Create 4–5 labels for each sample...
steps.instruction: Create 4–5 labels for each sample with the information supplied in step 4.

steps.title: Weigh the 7cm x 7cm paper...
steps.instruction: Weigh the 7cm x 7cm paper on the analytical scale and record the weight.

steps.title: Tare the piece of paper before adding the hair
steps.instruction: Tare the piece of paper before adding the hair.

steps.title: Carefully remove the hair sample from...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the hair sample from the container while wearing laboratory gloves. Identify the shaved end of the sample.

steps.title: Using scissors and a ruler cut 1-1
steps.instruction: Using scissors and a ruler cut 1-1.5cm of hair from the exposed hair. Depending upon the rate of hair growth for the specific species and individual, this will reflect hair cortisol from the most recent 1–1.5 months.

steps.title: Transfer the hair on the square...
steps.instruction: Transfer the hair on the square paper and weigh the cut sample. Record the weight.

steps.title: Use forceps to grab the hair...
steps.instruction: Use forceps to grab the hair and store it in the polypropylene tube.

steps.title: Place the label for that subject on the polypropylene tube
steps.instruction: Place the label for that subject on the polypropylene tube.

steps.title: Use the motorized pipette controller and...
steps.instruction: Use the motorized pipette controller and 10 mL serological pipette to add 5 mL of HPLC grade isopropanol to each 15 mL polypropylene tube.

steps.title: Invert the sample for 3 minutes...
steps.instruction: Invert the sample for 3 minutes on the rotator, using speed 12.

steps.title: Use a glass funnel to decant...
steps.instruction: Use a glass funnel to decant the isopropanol into a waste container.

steps.title: Carefully decant the isopropanol and use...
steps.instruction: Carefully decant the isopropanol and use a spatula to catch any hair that maybe traveling down the tube as you decant.

steps.title: Repeat steps 16–18 twice
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 16–18 twice.

steps.title: Set the tubes on a rack...
steps.instruction: Set the tubes on a rack in the fume hood with the lids off allowing the hair to completely dry. This will take 4 - 5 days.

steps.title: Separate each washed and dried hair...
steps.instruction: Separate each washed and dried hair sample in half and place each half into a mill jar. Grind the hair to a fine powder using the ball mill, for 6 minutes at 25 Hz.

steps.title: With the scapula scrape the sides...
steps.instruction: With the scapula scrape the sides of the jar to get all powdered hair free from the walls. Collect the hair and put it in the appropriate labeled microcentrifuge tube and weigh it.

steps.title: Place the sample ID label (step...
steps.instruction: Place the sample ID label (step 8) on a 2 mL microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Weigh the microcentrifuge tube (with the...
steps.instruction: Weigh the microcentrifuge tube (with the label affixed) , then tare.

steps.title: Clean the forceps with isopropanol before...
steps.instruction: Clean the forceps with isopropanol before using it again for the next hair sample.

steps.title: Once the appropriate amount has been...
steps.instruction: Once the appropriate amount has been placed in the microcentrifuge tube, store any remaining powdered hair in another labelled 2 mL microcentrifuge tube and place it in the refrigerator or at room temperature.

steps.title: The weight of powdered hair in...
steps.instruction: The weight of powdered hair in the microcentrifuge tube needed for the species is indicated below: Capuchins, marmosets and tamarins, 50mg (0.05g) Chimpanzees, 40mg (0.04g) It is essential to keep careful records of the subject ID and the weight of each sample.

steps.title: Use the motorized pipette controller and...
steps.instruction: Use the motorized pipette controller and a serological pipette to add 1 mL HPLC grade methanol to each microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Place the tube on a multi-purpose...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a multi-purpose tube rotator for 24 hours. Set the time for 24:00 (24 hours) and run at speed 12.

steps.title: Plan accordingly to be available to...
steps.instruction: Plan accordingly to be available to extract the methanol at the conclusion of the 24 hours.

steps.title: At the end of the 24...
steps.instruction: At the end of the 24 hours the samples must immediately be centrifuged at 14,000 RPM for 3 minutes.

steps.title: Collect 0
steps.instruction: Collect 0.6 mL (600 microliters) of supernatant into a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube. Do not disturb the pellet of hair.

steps.title: Steps 30–37 will take approximately 45 minutes - 1 hour
steps.instruction: Steps 30–37 will take approximately 45 minutes - 1 hour. Using the MicroVap underneath the fume hood turn on the nitrogen tank.

steps.title: Place each sample onto the bed...
steps.instruction: Place each sample onto the bed noting where each sample is placed.

steps.title: Position the nitrogen apparatus over the...
steps.instruction: Position the nitrogen apparatus over the top of the samples so the tips will be in the center of each microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Carefully lower until stop and lock into place
steps.instruction: Carefully lower until stop and lock into place.

steps.title: Turn the unit on
steps.instruction: Turn the unit on. Make small turns on the nitrogen gas knob on the side of the panel of the hood, also adjust the gauge of the MicroVap to be sure it is open.

steps.title: Slowly increase the amount of nitrogen...
steps.instruction: Slowly increase the amount of nitrogen being released using the knob on the hood until you see the gauge at approximately 10.

steps.title: Turning on the gas too fast...
steps.instruction: Turning on the gas too fast will cause the methanol in each sample to splash out. Going slower is better in order to prevent any samples from splashing methanol.

steps.title: Once the nitrogen steam is at...
steps.instruction: Once the nitrogen steam is at the desired intensity, wait for the evaporation to occur and keep monitoring the sample. Increase the flow of nitrogen as needed.

steps.title: The evaporation takes about 45 to...
steps.instruction: The evaporation takes about 45 to 1 hour depending on the intensity of the nitrogen steam. After use clean the metal pins with alcohol.

steps.title: Allow samples to thaw before diluting for the assay
steps.instruction: Allow samples to thaw before diluting for the assay.

steps.title: Dilute the sample
steps.instruction: Dilute the sample. Capuchins, marmosets, and tamarins, dilution is 1:40 Chimpanzees, no dilution is necessary

steps.title: Follow the procedure in the Salimetrics Manual for Salivary Cortisol
steps.instruction: Follow the procedure in the Salimetrics Manual for Salivary Cortisol.

steps.title: Repeat steps 9–15 for each sample
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 9–15 for each sample.

steps.title: Reconstitute each sample with PBS
steps.instruction: Reconstitute each sample with PBS.

steps.title: For capuchins, marmosets and tamarins, use...
steps.instruction: For capuchins, marmosets and tamarins, use 200 micrometers of PBS buffer. For chimpanzees, use 100 micrometers of PBS buffer.

steps.title: Cover the top of each microcentrifuge...
steps.instruction: Cover the top of each microcentrifuge tube with parafilm and store at -20 C until ready to perform assay.
